prompt:
Assign GRADE certainty ratings to each evidence item below.
Ratings: HIGH (systematic review of RCTs, low heterogeneity), MODERATE (RCT with some concerns, or consistent observational), LOW (observational studies, high heterogeneity, indirect evidence), VERY_LOW (case reports, expert opinion, serious limitations).

For each item, output ONLY the number and rating, one per line:
1. HIGH
2. MODERATE
...

EVIDENCE:
1. [SILVER] Source: The metabolic effects of intermittent versus continuous feed | Statement: Changes in amino-acid, fat and carbohydrate metabolites over time may modulate circadian rhythm, ghrelin, insulin resistance and autophagy.
2. [SILVER] Source: The metabolic effects of intermittent versus continuous feed | Statement: The Intermittent versus Continuous Feeding in Critically Ill Patients (NC...) study aimed to characterise these metabolite changes.
3. [SILVER] Source: The metabolic effects of intermittent versus continuous feed | Statement: The study set out to characterise changes in metabolite concentration over time under intermittent feeding.
4. [SILVER] Source: Objective assessment of drowsiness and reaction time during  | Statement: Ramadan fasting and its attendant lifestyle changes induce changes in circadian rhythm and associated physiological and metabolic functions.
5. [BRONZE] Source: Objective assessment of drowsiness and reaction time during  | Statement: Prior studies on psychomotor performance during Ramadan fasting have reported conflicting results.
